prompt:
Recommend the orchestrator (engine / model class / effort) for the NEXT session
of a VS Code extension session set. Answer with a concrete recommendation and a
one-paragraph rationale — this feeds an `ai-assignment.md` next-orchestrator note.

## Repo / workflow
Dabbler AI orchestration monorepo. Full-tier AI-led sessions: an orchestrator
does mechanical edits + git and delegates reasoning (architecture, verification)
through a cross-provider router. Engines available: claude (Anthropic), gpt-5-x
(OpenAI), gemini (Google). Model classes for claude: Opus (deep), Fable (fast/
capable), Sonnet, Haiku. Effort: low / medium / high.

## Set 094 (2 sessions) — "Getting Started shrink & manifest lifecycle"
Session 1 (JUST COMPLETED by claude / claude-opus-4-8 / high effort): rebuilt the
Getting Started webview form to two sections (Build project structure + Define
modules), retired the plan/session-set steps + New-module button + parallel
checkbox, added a shared create-on-demand `docs/modules.yaml` ensure-write
(atomic exclusive-create; five explicit-action call sites; never on activation)
+ a Work Explorer toolbar button, re-derived the completion-flag model, and
updated Layer-2/Layer-3 tests. Heavy trust-boundary + writer work; one routed
architecture ruling; suite green.

Session 2 (NEXT) scope: (a) a `dabbler.copyModuleDecompositionPrompt` command (a
bundled copy-prompt that instructs an AI to decompose the project into modules
and write modules.yaml) — the 4th ensure-write site referenced from the form +
manifest header; (b) shelve the parallel-session-sets UI with an escape hatch
(Command-Palette variant vs a `parallelHint` setting — decided via a routed
architecture call, then implemented); (c) a docs pass (quick-start, READMEs,
repository-reference, workflow doc, screenshots/strings) marking the compat
matrix shipped-state; (d) release prep for the SINGLE release boundary covering
Sets 091-094 (extension version bump + CHANGELOG; publish stays operator-gated);
plus the end-of-set change-log, Step 9 guidance review, and the armed advisory
path-aware critique. It is prompt-authoring + docs + release-prep heavy, less
novel-architecture heavy than S1, but it IS the release-boundary close (needs
care + a routed architecture call for the escape-hatch fork).

Recommend: engine, model class, effort. Note whether S1's orchestrator
(claude / Opus / high) should continue or whether a different class/effort fits
S2's docs+release character.
content:
### Recommendation

`claude` / `Fable` / `high`

### Rationale

We recommend `claude / Fable / high` effort for this session. While Session 1's heavy architectural lift justified the deep reasoning of Opus, Session 2's character is differentâ€”it's a broad-front push on documentation, prompt authoring, and meticulous release-prep, complemented by targeted implementation of a new command and a UI shelving decision. The `Fable` model class is the ideal fit, offering the necessary capability for the coding tasks and routed architecture implementation while being more efficient for the significant volume of prose and structured-text generation. The `high` effort level is retained from Session 1, not for architectural novelty, but to ensure the required diligence and breadth of execution across multiple domains for this critical release-boundary session, guaranteeing a high-quality, comprehensively documented release candidate.
